---
title: "Macro / Trade / Commodity Monitoring Brief"
subtitle: "U.S. net exports, real GDP, WTI crude, and FX pass-through to trade prices"
author: "Research-support pipeline `r-macro-trade-commodity-forecast`"
date: today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry:
      - margin=0.9in
    fontsize: 10pt
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(here)
  library(readr)
  library(dplyr)
  library(scales)
  library(knitr)
})

# Read the pipeline's published artifacts. The briefing reads the committed
# outputs rather than re-running the pipeline, so the memo is reproducible
# from whatever state `outputs/tables/` and `outputs/figures/` are in.
fc   <- readr::read_csv(here::here("outputs", "tables", "forecast_results.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)
mod  <- readr::read_csv(here::here("outputs", "tables", "model_summary.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)
pt   <- readr::read_csv(here::here("outputs", "tables", "passthrough_coefficients.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)

# Helpers used in inline `r ...` calls.
selected_model <- function(target_name) {
  unique(mod$model[mod$target == target_name])[1]
}
first_fc <- function(target_name) {
  fc |> dplyr::filter(target == target_name) |> dplyr::arrange(date) |> dplyr::slice(1)
}
last_fc <- function(target_name) {
  fc |> dplyr::filter(target == target_name) |> dplyr::arrange(date) |> dplyr::slice(dplyr::n())
}
```

## Headline

This is a one-page briefing template. It is **not** an official forecast or a
policy view. It is a draft research-support artifact rendered from the
pipeline's committed outputs in `outputs/tables/` and `outputs/figures/`.
A human reviewer must verify each claim against the source artifacts before
this memo is shared.

## Forecast summary

The pipeline produces 8-quarter `auto.arima` forecasts for three targets:
**net exports**, **real GDP**, and **WTI crude oil**. Models are selected
per target by `forecast::auto.arima`; selection can change across runs and
should be re-read each cycle from the panel below.

```{r forecast-table}
fc_tbl <- fc |>
  dplyr::group_by(target) |>
  dplyr::arrange(date, .by_group = TRUE) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    horizon_start = min(date),
    horizon_end   = max(date),
    point_first   = dplyr::first(point),
    point_last    = dplyr::last(point),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    model      = vapply(target, selected_model, character(1)),
    point_first = round(point_first, 2),
    point_last  = round(point_last, 2)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(target, model, horizon_start, horizon_end, point_first, point_last)

knitr::kable(
  fc_tbl,
  col.names = c("Target", "Selected model", "Horizon start",
                "Horizon end", "First-period point", "Last-period point"),
  caption = "Point forecasts at the first and last quarters of the 8-quarter horizon."
)
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Reading note.** `auto.arima` selects different specifications for each
target. A flat forecast (e.g. `ARIMA(0,1,0)`) means the model collapsed to a
random walk — a deliberate baseline, not a bug. Prediction intervals widen
as the horizon extends and are reported in `outputs/tables/forecast_results.csv`.
:::

## Forecast charts

::: {layout-ncol=3}
![Net exports](../outputs/figures/04_forecast_net_exports.png)

![Real GDP](../outputs/figures/05_forecast_real_gdp.png)

![WTI crude oil](../outputs/figures/07_forecast_oil_wti.png)
:::

## Exchange-rate pass-through to U.S. trade prices

The pipeline estimates a distributed-lag regression of Δlog(import deflator)
on Δlog(broad trade-weighted dollar) with 0–4 quarter lags and a CPI control.
The headline statistic is the **cumulative pass-through**: the sum of FX
coefficients across lags. The table below reports the latest estimate.

```{r pt-summary}
pt_summary <- pt |>
  dplyr::distinct(target, cumulative_passthrough, n_obs) |>
  dplyr::mutate(cumulative_passthrough = round(cumulative_passthrough, 3))

knitr::kable(
  pt_summary,
  col.names = c("Target deflator", "Cumulative pass-through", "Observations"),
  caption = "Cumulative pass-through coefficient (sum of FX coefficients across lags 0–4)."
)
```

![Δlog(USD index) vs. Δlog(U.S. import deflator)](../outputs/figures/06_fx_vs_import_deflator.png){width=70%}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Interpretation note.** Pass-through estimates describe an empirical
association between dollar movements and trade prices over the sample window.
They are not a causal decomposition. A negative coefficient on Δlog(USD)
indicates that a dollar appreciation is associated with falling U.S. import
prices in dollar terms, consistent with the standard producer-currency
pricing prior.
:::

## Macro / trade / commodity overview

The chart below shows the cleaned quarterly panel used by both the forecast
and pass-through stages.

![Quarterly panel — selected indicators](../outputs/figures/01_macro_trade_overview.png)

## Data, methodology, and review

- **Data.** All series are sourced from FRED via `fredr`. See
  [`README.md`](../README.md) for the full series list and
  [`docs/data_dictionary.md`](data_dictionary.md) for variable definitions.
- **Methodology.** See [`docs/methodology.md`](methodology.md) for the
  alignment rules, derived measures, model selection, and pass-through
  specification.
- **Research themes.** See [`docs/research_themes.md`](research_themes.md)
  for the questions this pipeline is designed to address.
- **Limitations.** ARIMA is a univariate baseline; the pass-through model is
  reduced-form and not a causal decomposition; quarterly aggregation
  discards intra-quarter variation in daily series (oil, FX, policy rate);
  the pipeline does not handle structural breaks (e.g., the 2020 shock)
  explicitly.

## How to render

```bash
# From the repository root:
quarto render docs/briefing_template.qmd --to html   # one-page HTML memo
quarto render docs/briefing_template.qmd --to pdf    # printable PDF (requires LaTeX)
```

The rendered output lands next to the `.qmd` file unless `--output-dir` is
passed. The briefing reads the committed `outputs/tables/` and
`outputs/figures/`; to refresh those, run
`source(here::here("scripts", "05_generate_outputs.R"))` first.

---

*Draft for human review. Not an official forecast or policy view.*
